---
title: "Projects"
---

# Project Name: Python Quiz App with AI Tutor

The **Python Quiz App** is an interactive learning tool that tests Python knowledge through multiple-choice questions. When users answer incorrectly, an AI model (running locally via Ollama) generates a simple explanation of why the correct answer is right. Questions come from a database, but users can also generate new questions on any Python topic using AI.

### Live Application

You can interact with the live system below.

<iframe src="https://python-quiz-app-tzkf.onrender.com/static/static.html" width="100%" height="700px" style="border:none;">

</iframe>

**View the code:** [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/susanmohammadk22/Python-Quiz-App) **Live Demo:** [Python Quiz App](https://python-quiz-app-tzkf.onrender.com/)

### Features

-   Multiple-choice Python questions from database
-   AI-generated explanations for wrong answers
-   Generate new questions on any Python topic
-   Tracks correct/incorrect score

### Technologies

-   **Backend:** FastAPI (Python)
-   **AI Engine:** Ollama + DeepSeek-Coder (local LLM)
-   **Database:** SQLite
-   **Frontend:** HTML/CSS/JavaScript

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Project Name: QIntel Application

The **QIntel Application** is a specialized system built with R and Shiny for event logic and response tracking. It utilizes a country detection algorithm and a SQLite backend to manage participant questions in real-time. It allows speakers to filter, classify, and display audience questions during live events.

### Live Application

You can interact with the live system below.

<iframe src="https://qevent.shinyapps.io/qevent_app/" width="100%" height="900px" style="border:none;">

</iframe>

**Read the full report:** [Final-Report.pdf](Final-Project.pdf)

**View the presentation:** [Presentation.pdf](Presentation.pdf) 

**Live demo:** [QIntel app](https://qevent.shinyapps.io/qevent_app/)

## AI Extension

I developed an AI module that:

-   Detects duplicate questions using text embeddings\
-   Classifies questions by topic\
-   Suggests answers based on historical responses\
-   Prevents repetitive submissions\
-   Displays questions and answers live on screen

### Why This Matters

Large meetings often suffer from repeated questions and missed answers. This system reduces redundancy, improves clarity, and enhances the experience for both speakers and participants.

### Technologies

-   **Frontend:** R + Shiny\
-   **Database:** SQLite / PostgreSQL\
-   **AI Engine:** Embedding models for semantic similarity\
-   **Documentation:** Quarto

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Flight Delay Prediction (DFW)

A predictive modeling project analyzing weather patterns, operational factors, and propagated delays to identify airport pairs that should not share a pilot due to weather-related delays.I used Lasso, Random Forest for methodologies for this project.

**Tech:** R, tidyverse, glmnet, randomForest

[GitHub Repository](https://github.com/susanmohammadk22/airline-weather-risk-analysis)

## NBA Player Stats & Gallery

This application provides a dynamic interface to explore NBA player data stored in a relational database. By integrating a SQLite backend with a Shiny front-end, I created a tool that allows for real-time data filtering and image rendering.

<iframe src="https://qevent.shinyapps.io/nba-final/" width="100%" height="600px" style="border:none;">

</iframe>

[View Source Code on GitHub](https://github.com/susanmohammadk22/NBA-Project)

**Technical Stack:** R (Shiny), SQLite, DT (DataTables), CSS.
